content: Optional RuleBattle Traits are optional rules that add additional flavour to a warband. All fighters must share this runemark to be eligible.There are also Battle Traits for each Grand Alliance. Trait RunemarkTraitDarkoath SavagersDarkoath SavagersSeeking Glory: After an enemy leader has been taken down by a melee attack action made by a friendly fighter with this battle trait, add two wild dice to your saved wild dice.

meta_description: Released: November 2021, Red Harvest boxed set. Refreshed August 2022.
content: RunemarkReactionDarkoath SavagersInstinctive Dodge: A fighter can make this reaction after they are targeted by an attack action, after the hit rolls have been made. Roll a dice for each critical hit. On a roll of 4+, that critical hit becomes a hit instead.

meta_description: Released: November 2021, Red Harvest boxed set. Refreshed August 2022.
content: RunemarkAbilityDarkoath Savagers[Double] A Vow Fulfilled: Until the end of this fighter's activation, each time an enemy fighter is taken down, you can pick either this fighter's Toughness characteristic or the Attacks or Strength characteristic of 1 weapon this fighter is armed with. Until the end of the battle, add 1 to the characteristic you picked.Darkoath SavagersMystic[Double] Visions of Glory: Roll a number of dice equal to the value of this ability. For each roll of 5+, add one wild dice to your saved wild dice.Darkoath SavagersHero[Double] Display of Brutality: A fighter can only use this ability if an enemy fighter has been taken down by an attack action made by them this activation. This fighter makes a bonus move action or a bonus attack action.Darkoath SavagersBerserker[Triple] Furious Rampage: Roll a dice for each visible enemy fighter within 3" of this fighter. On a roll of 3-4, allocate 1 damage point to the fighter being rolled for. On a roll of 5+, allocate a number of damage points to the fighter being rolled for equal to the value of this ability.Darkoath SavagersMystic[Quad] Curse of Withering: Pick a visible enemy fighter within 3" of this fighter and roll a number of dice equal to the value of this ability. For each roll of 4-5, until the end of the battle round, subtract 1 from that fighter's Toughness characteristic (to a minimum of 1). For each roll of 6, until the end of the battle, subtract 1 from that fighter's Toughness characteristic (to a minimum of 1).Darkoath Savagers[Quad] Deathblow: Add the value of this ability to the damage points allocated to enemy fighters by each hit and critical hit from the next melee attack action made by this fighter in this activation.

meta_description: Released: September 2024, Warhammer Community.
content: Optional RuleBattle Traits are optional rules that add additional flavour to a warband. All fighters must share this runemark to be eligible.Darkoath do not have their own Battle Trait, but can used the Grand Alliance Chaos trait instead. Trait RunemarkTraitChaosChaosAspire to Glory: Once per battle round, add 1 to the damage points allocated by the first critical hit from a melee attack action made by any friendly fighter with this battle trait that has a points cost of 125 or less.

meta_description: Released: September 2024, Warhammer Community.
content: RunemarkReactionfactions-chaos-darkoathGlorious Death: Fighters with the Beast (Beast) runemark cannot make this reaction. A fighter can make this reaction when they are targeted by a melee attack action, after the damage is totalled but before it is allocated to this fighter, if it is enough for this fighter to be taken down. Gain a [double] with a value of 1. If this fighter has the Hero runemark, gain a [triple] with a value of 1 instead. If this fighter is favoured, gain a [quad] with a value of 1 instead.

meta_description: Released: September 2024, Warhammer Community.
content: RunemarkAbilityfactions-chaos-darkoath[Double] Oath of Murder: Fighters with the Beast ( ) runemark cannot use this ability. After using this ability, this fighter is oathed for the rest of the battle. After an enemy fighter is taken down during an oathed fighter’s activation, that fighter is no longer oathed and is favoured for the rest of the battle. After a friendly fighter becomes favoured, gain a [double] with a value of 1.factions-chaos-darkoathScout[Double] Swift Attacker: This ability can only be used after an activation in which this fighter used a move action and that move ended within 1" of an enemy fighter. This fighter makes a bonus ranged attack action that must target an enemy fighter within 1" of them. If this fighter is favoured, add the value of this ability to the damage points allocated by each hit and critical hit from that ranged attack action.factions-chaos-darkoathDestroyer[Double] Raider’s Tenacity: Remove a number of damage points from this fighter up to half the value of this ability. If this fighter is favoured, remove an additional 3 damage points from this fighter.factions-chaos-darkoathFerocious[Tripe] Warleader: Pick a visible friendly fighter with the Darkoath (factions-chaos-darkoath) runemark within 6" of this fighter that has not activated yet this battle round. You can activate that fighter immediately after this fighter’s activation ends. If this fighter is favoured, pick up to 2 other friendly fighters with the Darkoath (factions-chaos-darkoath) runemark. Until the end of the battle round, each fighter you picked, including the first, can use the ‘Inspiring Presence’ ability without needing or using any ability dice.factions-chaos-darkoathBulwark[Triple] Rally the Tribes: Each friendly fighter with the Darkoath (factions-chaos-darkoath)) runemark and the Warrior (Warrior) runemark within 6" of this fighter makes a bonus move action. If this fighter is favoured, until the end of the battle round, each of those fighters also counts as 1 additional fighter when determining which player controls an objective.factions-chaos-darkoathBeast[Quad] Eye of the Dark Patron: You can pick a visible friendly fighter with the Darkoath (factions-chaos-darkoath)) runemark within 6" of this fighter that has not activated yet this battle round. You can activate that fighter immediately after this fighter’s activation ends. Then you can pick a visible enemy fighter within 6" of this fighter and roll 6 dice. For each 4+, allocate a number of damage points to that fighter equal to half the value of this ability (rounding up). In addition, add 2 to the Attacks characteristic of melee attack actions made by this fighter until the end of their activation.

meta_description: Released: September 2024, Warhammer Community.
content: RunemarkAbilityfactions-chaos-darkoathBerserker[Triple] Carve a Path to Glory: Until the end of this fighter’s activation, add half the value of this ability (rounding up) to the Attacks characteristic of melee attack actions made by this fighter. If this fighter is favoured, add the value of this ability to the Attacks characteristic of melee attack actions made by this fighter instead.factions-chaos-darkoathAgile[Triple] Uncanny Shot: Until the end of this fighter’s activation, add half the value of this ability (rounding up) to the Attacks characteristic of ranged attack actions made by this fighter. While this fighter is favoured and/or targeting an enemy fighter within 1" of a friendly Gunnar Brand, add the value of this ability to the Attacks characteristic of ranged attack actions made by this fighter instead.factions-chaos-darkoathElite[Triple] Warqueen’s Fury: This fighter makes a number of bonus melee attack actions equal to the number of enemy fighters within 1" of them. If this fighter is favoured, they make a number of bonus melee attack actions equal to the number of enemy fighters within 3" of them instead and the Range characteristic of those bonus attack actions is increased to 3. In both cases, each bonus attack action must target a different enemy fighter, even if that enemy fighter is more than 1" from this fighter but so long as each enemy fighter within 1" of this fighter has been targeted first.factions-chaos-darkoathMystic[Triple] Withering Death: Pick a visible enemy fighter within 9" of this fighter and roll 6 dice. For each 4+, allocate a number of damage points to that fighter equal to half the value of this ability (rounding up). If this fighter is favoured, roll 9 dice instead of 6.factions-chaos-darkoathBrute[Triple] Demolishing Strikes: Until the end of this fighter’s activation, melee attack actions made by this fighter score a hit on a 3+. If this fighter is favoured, in addition, until the end of this fighter’s activation, melee attack actions made by this fighter score a critical hit on a 5+.

meta_description: Released: September 2024, Warhammer Community.
content: Gunnar Brand is chieftain of the Brand clan, a tribe of Darkoath renowned for their tenacity and talent for slaughter. Charging into battle alongside his oathsworn kin, Gunnar hacks his enemies to pieces with his twin axes, each ruined corpse offered up as tribute to the Chaos Gods in return for dark blessings.

meta_description: Released: September 2024, Warhammer Community.
content: This document includes additional fighter profiles for the Darkoath faction as well as abilities specific to those fighters. The Darkoath belong to Grand Alliance Chaos (Chaos).

meta_description: Released: September 2024, Warhammer Community.
content: When picking a warband, the following rule is in addition to the bullet points under ‘The Warbands’ in the core rules (Core Book, pg 60) and ‘Step 1 – Choose Your Faction and Fighters’ in the core rules (Core Book, pg 99). Each fighter in this download can only be included once in your warband.

meta_description: Source: Rot and Ruin (out of print)
content: D6Origin1Exiled Order: These warriors belonged to the same cadre of alchemists, driven out of the free cities due to their disturbing interests.2Rotting Away: The body parts and skin of these warriors are slowly sloughing away, much to their own grotesque fascination.3Abductors: Whenever the Creed needs living subjects for its experiments, these warriors are sent out to find them.4Lurkers in the Mire: These warriors are used to spending days out in the bubbling filth of the Rotmire seeking foul, blight-ridden ingredients.5Addicted to Decay: These warriors desire to contract as many hideous ailments as possible, believing each pox makes them stronger.6Joyful Company: These cultists approach even the most gruesome task with an eerie good humour.

meta_description: Source: Rot and Ruin (out of print)
content: D6Background1Chirurgeon: This warrior is an artist with the blade, able to extract organs from their victims without damaging them in the process.2Deranged Ingenuity: Even their fellow cultists are unnerved by this warrior's truly nightmarish experiments.3Deluded Genius: This warrior still insists they serve the greater good of humanity, even as their victims writhe and scream in agony.4Perfectionist: This warrior is enraged when anything disrupts their carefully laid plans.5Disdainful of Warriors: This warrior considers those with live by the sword to be little6Cannot Abide Waste: This warrior always harvests the organs and fluids of those they have wounded or slain.

meta_description: The diabolical schemes of the skaven are a blight on the Mortal Realms. These devious ratmen are utterly convinced
content: Optional RuleBattle Traits are optional rules that add additional flavour to a warband. All fighters must share this runemark to be eligible.There are also Battle Traits for each Grand Alliance. Trait RunemarkTraitSkavenSkavenThere are Always More: At the end of the battle rounds 2 and 3, you can pick a friendly fighter with this battle trait that is taken down and has a points cost of 70 or less. At the start of the next battle round, you can place that fighter on a platform or on the battlefield floor, more than 6” from each enemy fighter, objective and treasure token, and within 3” of one or more battlefield edges.

meta_description: The diabolical schemes of the skaven are a blight on the Mortal Realms. These devious ratmen are utterly convinced
content: RunemarkReactionSkavenMusk of Fear: A fighter can make this reaction after an enemy fighter finishes a move action visible to this fighter and within 3" of this fighter. Pick another friendly fighter with the Skaven (Skaven) faction runemark within 3" of this fighter. That fighter makes a bonus move action or a bonus disengage action, and must end that action more than 3" away from the enemy fighter that made the move action.

meta_description: The diabolical schemes of the skaven are a blight on the Mortal Realms. These devious ratmen are utterly convinced
The diabolical schemes of the skaven are a blight on the Mortal Realms. These devious ratmen are utterly convinced
content: RunemarkAbilitySkavenAgile[Double] Scurry Away: A fighter can only use this ability if they are within 3" of an enemy fighter. Roll a dice. On a roll of 2+, this fighter makes a bonus disengage action.SkavenFrenzied[Double] Crack the Whip: Pick a visible friendly fighter with the Beast (Beast) runemark within 4" of this fighter. That fighter makes a bonus melee attack action.SkavenElite[Double] Hired Bodyguard: Until the end of the battle round, friendly fighters with the Hero (Hero) runemark cannot be targeted while they are within 1" of this fighter.SkavenBruteBeast[Double] Unleashed Warp-fury: This fighter makes a bonus attack action. At the end of this fighter's activation, allocate a number of damage points to this fighter equal to the number of damage points allocated to this fighter.SkavenBrute[Triple] Crushing Charge: Until the end of this fighter's activation, the next time this fighter finishes a move action within 1" of an enemy fighter, pick a visible enemy fighter within 1" of this fighter. Allocate a number of damage points to that fighter equal to the value of this ability.SkavenBerserker[Triple] Poisonous Fumes: Roll a dice for each visible enemy fighter within 3" of this fighter. On a roll of 3+, allocate a number of damage points equal to half the value of this ability (rounding up) to that fighter.SkavenDestroyer[Quad] Expend Warpstone Spark Canister: This fighter makes a bonus attack action. In addition, add half the value of this ability (rounding up) to the damage points allocated to enemy fighters by each hit and critical hit from that attack action.

meta_description: The diabolical schemes of the skaven are a blight on the Mortal Realms. These devious ratmen are utterly convinced
content: RunemarkAbilitySkavenHeroMystic[Double] Consume Warpstone Token: Roll a number of dice equal to the value of this ability. For each roll of 1, allocate 1 damage point to this fighter. For each roll of 4+, add 1 to the damage points allocated to enemy fighters by each hit or critical hit from the next missile attack action made by this fighter in this activation.SkavenHeroDestroyer[Double] Warp Lightning: Pick a number of enemy fighters up to the value of this ability. The first fighter picked must be within 6" of, and visible to, this fighter and each subsequent fighter picked must be within 3" of, and visible to, a fighter that has already been picked. The same fighter cannot be picked more than once. Allocate 1 damage point to each fighter picked.SkavenHeroFrenzied[Double] Flesh-mend: Pick a visible friendly fighter with the Beast (Beast) runemark within 4" of this fighter. Remove a number of damage points allocated to that fighter up to half the value of this ability (rounding up).SkavenHeroScout[Triple] Running Death: This fighter makes a bonus move action or bonus disengage action. In addition, until the end of this fighter's activation, add 1 to the damage points allocated to enemy fighters by each critical hit from attack actions made by this fighter.SkavenHero[Triple] Lead from the Back: Until the end of the battle round, add 1 to the Attacks characteristic of melee attack actions made by visible friendly fighters while they are within 6" of this fighter.SkavenHeroPriest[Triple] Recite from the Book of Woes: Until the end of the battle round, enemy fighters cannot use abilities while they are within 3" of this fighter.SkavenHeroMount[Triple] Forward, Minions!: Pick a visible friendly fighter with the Skaven (Skaven) runemark and that does not have the Hero (Hero) or Monster (Monster) runemark wholly within 6" of this fighter. That fighter can make a bonus attack action and a bonus move action up to a number of inches equal to the value of this ability. If an enemy fighter is not taken down by damage points allocated by that attack action, or if that move action does not end within 1" of an enemy fighter, allocate D6 damage points to that friendly fighter.Designer's Note: They are punished for their incompetence!SkavenHeroDestroyer[Triple] Warpstone Sights: Pick a visible enemy fighter within 18" of this fighter. For the rest of this fighter's activation, subtract the value of this ability from the Toughness characteristic of that enemy fighter (to a minimum of 1). If this fighter has not moved this activation and the value of this ability is equal to or greater than the unmodified Toughness characteristic of that enemy fighter, count each hit scored from the next ranged attack action made by this fighter as a critical hit instead. Before the Total Damage step for each of this fighter's ranged attack actions this activation, this fighter makes a bonus move action up to a number of inches equal to half the value of this ability, directly away from the target of that ranged attack and as if they were jumping.

meta_description: The diabolical schemes of the skaven are a blight on the Mortal Realms. These devious ratmen are utterly convinced
content: RunemarkAbilitySkavenSpiteclaw's SwarmHero[Triple] There Are Always More: Pick a friendly fighter with both the Spiteclaw's Swarm runemark (Spiteclaw's Swarm) and the Minion runemark (Minion) that has been taken down. Set up that fighter once more on the battlefield wholly within 3" of this fighter. The fighter set up on the battlefield no longer counts as been taken down. Remove a number of damage points allocated to that fighter up to the value of this ability.

meta_description: The diabolical schemes of the skaven are a blight on the Mortal Realms. These devious ratmen are utterly convinced
content: RunemarkAbility[Double] Way of the Slinking Rat: A fighter can only use this ability if they are within 3" of an enemy fighter. Roll a dice. On a roll of 2+, this fighter can make a bonus disengage action.Champion[Triple] Spitting Cobra Technique: Pick an enemy fighter within 6" of this fighter and roll a number of dice equal to the value of this ability. For each roll that equals or exceeds that enemy fighter's Toughness characteristic, allocate 3 damage points to that enemy fighter.Hero[Quad] Way of the Hidden Paw: This fighter can make a bonus move action. Then this fighter can make a bonus attack action. Add X to the Attacks characteristic of that bonus attack action, where X is the number of friendly fighters with the Skittershank's Clawpack () runemark within 1" of the target.

meta_description: The diabolical schemes of the skaven are a blight on the Mortal Realms. These devious ratmen are utterly convinced
content: RunemarkAbilitySkabbik's PlaguepackTerrifying[Double] Befoul the Land: Pick an objective within 3" of this fighter or a treasure token within 3" of this fighter. That treasure token can be one that is being carried by a fighter if that fighter is visible to this fighter. Roll a dice at the end of the battle round for each enemy fighter within 3" of that objective or treasure token. On a roll of 3+, allocate D3 damage points for the fighter being rolled for. The same objective or treasure token cannot be picked more than once per battle round.Skabbik's Plaguepack[Double] Disgusting Appearance: Until the end of the battle round, subtract 1 from the Attacks characteristic (to a minimum of 1) of melee attack actions made by enemy fighters that target this fighter.Skabbik's PlaguepackHero[Triple] Unctuous Citations: Pick a friendly fighter with the Skabbik's Plaguepack (Skabbik's Plaguepack) runemark that is within 7" of this fighter. This fighter can make a bonus move action or a bonus attack action, and then the fighter you picked can make the same bonus action.Skabbik's PlaguepackHero[Quad] Manifestation of a Great Plague: Pick this fighter, or pick a number of objectives visible to this fighter equal to half the value of this ability (rounding up). Allocate a number of damage points equal to the value of this ability to all enemy fighters within 3" of either this fighter or those objectives.

meta_description: The diabolical schemes of the skaven are a blight on the Mortal Realms. These devious ratmen are utterly convinced
content: RunemarkAbilityZikkit's Tunnelpack[Double] Tunnel-skitter: Pick a point on the battlefield floor or a platform within 9" of this fighter. Then, remove this fighter from the battlefield and set them up within 1" of that point and more than 5" from all enemy fighters.Zikkit's Tunnelpack[Double] Tick Tick Tick…: If your Tik Tik is taken down and is listed on your warband roster, set up a friendly Tik Tik within 1" of this fighter. That Tik Tik cannot move this turn.Zikkit's TunnelpackBeast[Double] Kaboom!: Allocate D3 damage points to each enemy fighter within 3" of this fighter and gain 1 wild dice. Then, remove this fighter from the battlefield (this fighter is taken down).Zikkit's TunnelpackChampion[Triple] Vrrrrrrmmmmmm!: If this fighter is within 3" of the battlefield edge, remove this fighter from the battlefield, then roll a dice. Then, set them up on the battlefield floor, within 3" of the battlefield edge and more than X" from all enemy fighters, where X is equal to the roll.Zikkit's TunnelpackHero[Quad] Drill-drill the Fool-Foe!: Pick a visible enemy fighter within 1" of this fighter and roll 6 dice. For each roll that is equal to or less than the value of this ability, allocate 1 damage point to that enemy fighter, subtract 1 from the value of this ability and roll those dice again. Keep rolling in this way until the value of this ability is 0 or none of the dice rolled are equal to or less than the value of this ability.

Bladeborn fightersSpiteclaw's Swarm are from Warhammer Underworlds, so adhere to Bladeborn rules.

content: The diseased skaven of Skabbik's Plaguepack plan to usher in the birth of one of the Great Plagues – an apocalyptic disease prophecised amongst the fanatical verminkin of the Clans Pestilens. Their foulsome quest has taken the Plaguepack deep into the heart of Wyrdhollow, to seek one of the arcane ingredients in this foul brew. Should Skabbik's noisome flock succeed in their task, great swathes of the Mortal Realms may be reduced to rancid rotting mulch. Let their enemies beware – a sickening death awaits any who stand against the children of the Great Corruptor. Bladeborn fightersSkabbik's Plaguepack are from Warhammer Underworlds, so adhere to Bladeborn rules.

content: Q: How does the Recite from the Book of Woes ability work? A: Until the end of the round, enemy fighters cannot use abilities while they are within 3" of the fighter that used the ability. This has no effect on abilities used by fighters more than 3" from the fighter that used the ability. It also has no effect on reactions.

meta_description: Those mortals dedicated to Slaanesh are amongst the most unnerving servants of all the Dark Gods. In their pursuit of sensation, these scions of torment gleefully commit the most terrible acts imaginable. Yet this contentment withers away as swiftly as it comes, and so the Sybarites must ply their agonising talents far and wide.
content: Optional RuleBattle Traits are optional rules that add additional flavour to a warband. All fighters must share this runemark to be eligible.There are also Battle Traits for each Grand Alliance. Trait RunemarkTraitSlaanesh SybaritesHedonites of Slaanesh: SybaritesPain-fuelled Rapture: After a friendly fighter with this battle trait is allocated one or more damage points by an attack action or reaction, you can choose for that fighter to enter a pain-fuelled rapture. If they do so, allocate 3 damage points to that friendly fighter, and then add 2 to the Attacks characteristic of the next attack action made by this fighter in this battle round.

meta_description: Those mortals dedicated to Slaanesh are amongst the most unnerving servants of all the Dark Gods. In their pursuit of sensation, these scions of torment gleefully commit the most terrible acts imaginable. Yet this contentment withers away as swiftly as it comes, and so the Sybarites must ply their agonising talents far and wide.
content: RunemarkReactionSlaanesh SybaritesShared Pain: A fighter can make this reaction after they are targeted by a melee attack action but before the hit rolls are made. For each hit roll from that attack action that hits, allocate 1 damage point to the attacking fighter. For each critical hit, allocate 2 damage points to the attacking fighter instead.

meta_description: Those mortals dedicated to Slaanesh are amongst the most unnerving servants of all the Dark Gods. In their pursuit of sensation, these scions of torment gleefully commit the most terrible acts imaginable. Yet this contentment withers away as swiftly as it comes, and so the Sybarites must ply their agonising talents far and wide.

Those mortals dedicated to Slaanesh are amongst the most unnerving servants of all the Dark Gods. In their pursuit of sensation, these scions of torment gleefully commit the most terrible acts imaginable. Yet this contentment withers away as swiftly as it comes, and so the Sybarites must ply their agonising talents far and wide.
content: RunemarkAbilitySlaanesh Sybarites[Double] Unrivalled Velocity: Add 3 to this fighter's Move characteristic for their next move action in this activation.Slaanesh SybaritesWarrior[Double] Dance of the Wailing Blade: Until the end of this fighter's activation, add 1 to the damage points allocated to enemy fighters by each hit and critical hit from melee attack actions made by this fighter.Slaanesh SybaritesFerocious[Double] Slaves to Impulse: A fighter can only use this ability if there is a visible enemy fighter within 6" of them. This fighter makes a bonus move action and must finish closer to the closest visible enemy fighter than they were at the start of that move action.Slaanesh SybaritesDestroyer[Triple] Impaling Strike: Until the end of this fighter's activation, the next time this fighter finishes a move action within 1" of an enemy fighter, pick a visible enemy fighter within 1" of this fighter. Allocate a number of damage points to that fighter equal to the value of this ability.Slaanesh SybaritesMinion[Triple] Volley of Tearing Blades: Add 2 to the Attacks characteristic of the next missile attack action made by this fighter in this activation. In addition, add 1 to the Strength characteristic of that action while this fighter is within 3" of a friendly fighter with the Hedonites of Slaanesh: Sybarites (Slaanesh Sybarites) faction runemark and the Priest (Priest) runemark.Slaanesh SybaritesBerserker[Quad] Ego-driven Excess: This fighter can make a bonus move action or a bonus attack action. In addition, each time a fighter is taken down by an attack action made by this fighter this activation, this fighter can make a further bonus move action or bonus attack action.

meta_description: Those mortals dedicated to Slaanesh are amongst the most unnerving servants of all the Dark Gods. In their pursuit of sensation, these scions of torment gleefully commit the most terrible acts imaginable. Yet this contentment withers away as swiftly as it comes, and so the Sybarites must ply their agonising talents far and wide.
content: RunemarkAbilitySlaanesh SybaritesHero[Double] Violence and Excess: A fighter can only use this ability if an enemy fighter has been taken down by an attack action made by them this activation. This fighter makes a bonus move action or a bonus attack action.Slaanesh SybaritesBulwark[Double] You First, I Insist: Pick a visible enemy fighter within 1" of this fighter. That enemy fighter makes a bonus attack action targeting this fighter. Until the end of this f ighter's activation: Add 2 to the Attacks and Strength characteristics of melee attack actions made by this fighter that target that enemy fighter.Add 3 to the damage points allocated by each critical hit from melee attack actions made by this fighter that target that enemy fighter.Slaanesh SybaritesHeroMystic[Triple] Tendrils of Unnatural Smoke: Until the end of the battle round, subtract 1 from the Attacks characteristic (to a minimum of 1) of attack actions made by enemy fighters while they are within 6" of this fighter.Slaanesh SybaritesHeroChampion[Quad] Paragon of Depravity: Until the end of the battle round, add the value of this ability to the Strength characteristic of melee attack actions made by visible friendly fighters while they are within 6" of this fighter.

meta_description: Those mortals dedicated to Slaanesh are amongst the most unnerving servants of all the Dark Gods. In their pursuit of sensation, these scions of torment gleefully commit the most terrible acts imaginable. Yet this contentment withers away as swiftly as it comes, and so the Sybarites must ply their agonising talents far and wide.
content: RunemarkAbilityThe Dread Pageant[Quad] Cavalcade of Madness: This fighter makes a bonus attack action. In addition, if either this fighter or the target fighter has any damage points allocated to them, add 1 to the Attacks characteristic of that attack action. If both this fighter and the target fighter have any damage points allocated to them, add 3 to the Attacks characteristic of that attack action instead.

content: Led by Vasillac the Gifted, a former warrior of the Myrmidesh who now seeks his own glory, the Dread Pageant have brough terrible suffering to the living mountain of Beastgrave. In battle, their manifold skills combine into one blinding storm of blades. Bladeborn fightersThe Dread Pageant are from Warhammer Underworlds, so adhere to Bladeborn rules.

meta_description: From the shadows of civilisation, the Arcanite cabals weave sinister plots and twist the strands of fate according to the desires of their master, Tzeentch. When called to war, throngs of sorcerous cultists fight alongside trilling beastkin and mutated mortal champions, stoking the flames of change through their arcane mastery.
content: Optional RuleBattle Traits are optional rules that add additional flavour to a warband. All fighters must share this runemark to be eligible.There are also Battle Traits for each Grand Alliance. Trait RunemarkTraitTzeentch ArcanitesDisciples of Tzeentch: ArcanitesWarp Fate: After the initiative phase, starting with the attacker, each player of a warband with this battle trait can roll one dice for each [double], [triple], or [quad] they have. On a 4+, change the value of those ability dice to 6.

meta_description: From the shadows of civilisation, the Arcanite cabals weave sinister plots and twist the strands of fate according to the desires of their master, Tzeentch. When called to war, throngs of sorcerous cultists fight alongside trilling beastkin and mutated mortal champions, stoking the flames of change through their arcane mastery.
content: RunemarkReactionTzeentch ArcanitesTwist of Fate: A fighter can make this reaction after they are targeted by an attack action but before the hit rolls are made. Pick one of the hit rolls, and change the value of one of your [doubles] to that value.

meta_description: From the shadows of civilisation, the Arcanite cabals weave sinister plots and twist the strands of fate according to the desires of their master, Tzeentch. When called to war, throngs of sorcerous cultists fight alongside trilling beastkin and mutated mortal champions, stoking the flames of change through their arcane mastery.

From the shadows of civilisation, the Arcanite cabals weave sinister plots and twist the strands of fate according to the desires of their master, Tzeentch. When called to war, throngs of sorcerous cultists fight alongside trilling beastkin and mutated mortal champions, stoking the flames of change through their arcane mastery.
content: RunemarkAbilityTzeentch Arcanites[Double] Uttered Words of Arcane Power: Add 1 to the Attacks and Strength characteristics of the next melee attack action made by this fighter in this activation.Tzeentch ArcanitesBrute[Double] Vicious Beak: Pick a visible enemy fighter within 1" of this fighter and roll a number of dice equal to the value of this ability. For each roll of 4+, allocate 1 damage point to that fighter.Tzeentch ArcanitesScout[Double] Vulcharc: Pick an enemy fighter within 20" of this fighter. Until the end of the battle round, that fighter cannot make disengage actions.Tzeentch ArcanitesFrenzied[Triple] Guided by the Future: Add half the value of this ability (rounding up) to the damage points allocated to enemy fighters by each hit and critical hit from the next attack action made by this fighter in this activation that targets an enemy fighter that has not activated this battle round.Tzeentch ArcanitesDestroyer[Triple] Guided by the Past: Add the value of this ability to the damage points allocated to enemy fighters by each hit and critical hit from the next melee attack action made by this fighter in this activation that targets an enemy fighter that has activated this battle round.

meta_description: From the shadows of civilisation, the Arcanite cabals weave sinister plots and twist the strands of fate according to the desires of their master, Tzeentch. When called to war, throngs of sorcerous cultists fight alongside trilling beastkin and mutated mortal champions, stoking the flames of change through their arcane mastery.
content: RunemarkAbilityTzeentch ArcanitesHero[Double] Locus of Sorcery: Add half the value of this ability (rounding up) to the Strength characteristic of the next missile attack action made by this fighter in this activation.Tzeentch ArcanitesHeroFerocious[Double] Brutal Rage: A fighter can only use this ability if 15 or more damage points have been allocated to them. Until the end of the battle, add 1 to the Attacks characteristic of melee attack actions made by this fighter.Tzeentch ArcanitesHero[Triple] Master of Destiny: Until the end of the battle round, add the value of this ability to the value of other abilities (to a maximum of 6) used by friendly fighters within 12" of this fighter.Tzeentch ArcanitesHeroBerserker[Triple] Warptongue Blade: Pick a visible enemy fighter within 1" of this fighter and roll a number of dice equal to the value of this ability. For each roll of 2-5, allocate 1 damage point to that fighter. For each roll of 6, allocate 3 damage points to that fighter.Tzeentch ArcanitesHeroChampion[Quad] Lord of Fate: Until the end of the battle round, add 1 to the Attacks characteristic of melee attack actions made by visible friendly fighters while they are within 9" of this fighter.

meta_description: From the shadows of civilisation, the Arcanite cabals weave sinister plots and twist the strands of fate according to the desires of their master, Tzeentch. When called to war, throngs of sorcerous cultists fight alongside trilling beastkin and mutated mortal champions, stoking the flames of change through their arcane mastery.
content: RunemarkAbilityEyes of the NineWarrior[Double] Split Into Brimstone Horrors: A fighter can use this ability only if they have 1 or more damage points allocated to them. Set up 1 new fighter with both the Eyes of the Nine runemark (Eyes of the Nine) and the Minion runemark (Minion) on the battlefield within 3" of this fighter. Then, this fighter is taken down.Eyes of the NineElite[Triple] Cleaving Strike: Add half the value of this ability (rounding up) to the number of damage points allocated by each hit and critical hit from the next attack action made by this fighter this activation that has a Range characteristic of 3 or less.Eyes of the Nine[Quad] Empowered Bolts: This fighter makes a bonus attack action. In addition, if that attack action has the Blast runemark (Blast), add half the value of this ability (rounding up) to the Attacks and Strength characteristic of that attack action.

content: Agents of the Gaunt Summoners, the Eyes of the Nine seek out artefacts suffused with realm magics that they can corrupt with Tzeentchian energy, and with these ensorcelled lodestones plot to tether the Silver Towers to reality. Bladeborn fightersThe Eyes of the Nine are from Warhammer Underworlds, so adhere to Bladeborn rules.

meta_description: Quicksilver fast, the Hedonites of Slaanesh: Daemons are driven to a manic state of ecstasy by the myriad sensations only battle can provide. Each is a shard of the Dark Prince devoted to spreading his creed throughout the realms. The feeling of flesh parting under the caress of their razor-sharp claws is a rapturous wonder to these creatures, and where they tread, the battlefield is filled with wicked laughter as the daemons are energised by the arterial spray of hot blood.
content: Optional RuleBattle Traits are optional rules that add additional flavour to a warband. All fighters must share this runemark to be eligible.There are also Battle Traits for each Grand Alliance. Trait RunemarkTraitSlaanesh DaemonsHedonites of Slaanesh: DaemonsLure of the Missing God: After the initiative phase, you can pick an enemy fighter within 1” of one of more friendly fighters with this battle trait and then roll a dice. On a roll of 3+, until the end of the battle round, that fighter cannot make disengage actions.

meta_description: Quicksilver fast, the Hedonites of Slaanesh: Daemons are driven to a manic state of ecstasy by the myriad sensations only battle can provide. Each is a shard of the Dark Prince devoted to spreading his creed throughout the realms. The feeling of flesh parting under the caress of their razor-sharp claws is a rapturous wonder to these creatures, and where they tread, the battlefield is filled with wicked laughter as the daemons are energised by the arterial spray of hot blood.
content: RunemarkReactionSlaanesh DaemonsShared Pain: A fighter can make this reaction after they are targeted by a melee attack action but before the hit rolls are made. For each hit roll from that attack action that hits, allocate 1 damage point to the attacking fighter. For each critical hit, allocate 2 damage points to the attacking fighter instead.

meta_description: Quicksilver fast, the Hedonites of Slaanesh: Daemons are driven to a manic state of ecstasy by the myriad sensations only battle can provide. Each is a shard of the Dark Prince devoted to spreading his creed throughout the realms. The feeling of flesh parting under the caress of their razor-sharp claws is a rapturous wonder to these creatures, and where they tread, the battlefield is filled with wicked laughter as the daemons are energised by the arterial spray of hot blood.

Quicksilver fast, the Hedonites of Slaanesh: Daemons are driven to a manic state of ecstasy by the myriad sensations only battle can provide. Each is a shard of the Dark Prince devoted to spreading his creed throughout the realms. The feeling of flesh parting under the caress of their razor-sharp claws is a rapturous wonder to these creatures, and where they tread, the battlefield is filled with wicked laughter as the daemons are energised by the arterial spray of hot blood.
content: RunemarkAbilitySlaanesh Daemons[Double] Lithe and Swift: Add 3 to this fighter's Move characteristic for their next move action in this activation.Slaanesh DaemonsWarrior[Double] Sadistic Killers: Until the end of this fighter's activation, add 1 to the damage points allocated to enemy fighters by each hit and critical hit from melee attack actions made by this fighter.Slaanesh DaemonsAgile[Double] Crushing Grip: Pick a visible enemy fighter within 1" of this fighter and roll a dice. On a roll of 2+, until the end of the battle round, that fighter cannot make move actions or disengage actions.Slaanesh DaemonsDestroyer[Triple] Impaling Strike: Until the end of this fighter's activation, the next time this fighter finishes a move action within 1" of an enemy fighter, pick a visible enemy fighter within 1" of this fighter. Allocate a number of damage points to that fighter equal to the value of this ability.Slaanesh DaemonsAgile[Triple] Deadly Venom: Add half the value of this ability (rounding up) to the damage points allocated to enemy fighters by each hit and critical hit from the next melee attack action made by this fighter in this activation.

meta_description: Quicksilver fast, the Hedonites of Slaanesh: Daemons are driven to a manic state of ecstasy by the myriad sensations only battle can provide. Each is a shard of the Dark Prince devoted to spreading his creed throughout the realms. The feeling of flesh parting under the caress of their razor-sharp claws is a rapturous wonder to these creatures, and where they tread, the battlefield is filled with wicked laughter as the daemons are energised by the arterial spray of hot blood.
content: RunemarkAbilitySlaanesh DaemonsHeroMystic[Double] Discordant Disruption: Until the end of the battle round, subtract 1 from the Attacks characteristic (to a minimum of 1) of attack actions made by enemy fighters while they are within 6" of this fighter.Slaanesh DaemonsHero[Triple] Locus of Excruciation: Until the end of the battle round, add 1 to the Strength characteristic of melee attack actions made by visible friendly fighters while they are within 6" of this fighter.Slaanesh DaemonsHeroTerrifying[Quad] Mirror of Absorption: Pick a visible enemy fighter within 9" of this fighter. That fighter cannot activate or make reactions in this battle round.

content: RunemarkReactionimage2vector-4Triumphant Tittering: A fighter can make this reaction without forfeiting one of their actions in that battle round, and can make this reaction even if they already activated this battle round. A fighter can make this reaction after another friendly fighter with the Thricefold Discord (image2vector-4) runemark is slain. This fighter makes a bonus move action or a bonus attack action.

meta_description: The Disciples of Tzeentch: Daemons are as bizarre and unpredictable as the god from whom they were spawned. Capering across the battlefield in a riot of colour and magic, they overwhelm their foes with sizzling spell-bolts, lashing talons and gouts of blistering wyrdflame, exulting as their hapless victims are consumed by the fires of change.
content: Optional RuleBattle Traits are optional rules that add additional flavour to a warband. All fighters must share this runemark to be eligible.There are also Battle Traits for each Grand Alliance. Trait RunemarkTraitTzeentch DaemonsDisciples of Tzeentch: DaemonsWarp Destiny: After the initiative phase, each player whose warband has this battle trait can pick two any [double], [triple] or [quad] they have. Swap the values of those ability dice.

meta_description: The Disciples of Tzeentch: Daemons are as bizarre and unpredictable as the god from whom they were spawned. Capering across the battlefield in a riot of colour and magic, they overwhelm their foes with sizzling spell-bolts, lashing talons and gouts of blistering wyrdflame, exulting as their hapless victims are consumed by the fires of change.
content: RunemarkReactionTzeentch DaemonsTwist of Fate: A fighter can make this reaction after they are targeted by an attack action but before the hit rolls are made. Pick one of the hit rolls, and change the value of one of your [doubles] to that value.Tzeentch DaemonsChampionSplit: A fighter can make this reaction when they are targeted by an attack action, after the damage is totalled but before it is allocated to this fighter. Set up 2 new fighters with the Disciples of Tzeentch: Daemons (Tzeentch Daemons) faction runemark and the Warrior (Warrior) runemark on the battlefield within 3" of this fighter. Then, this fighter is taken down. In a campaign battle, do not make an injury roll for this fighter.Tzeentch DaemonsWarriorSplit Again: A fighter can make this reaction when they are targeted by an attack action, after the damage is totalled but before it is allocated to this fighter. Set up 1 new fighter with the Disciples of Tzeentch: Daemons (Tzeentch Daemons) faction runemark and the Minion (Minion) runemark on the battlefield within 3" of this fighter. Then, this fighter is taken down. In a campaign battle, do not make an injury roll for this fighter.

meta_description: The Disciples of Tzeentch: Daemons are as bizarre and unpredictable as the god from whom they were spawned. Capering across the battlefield in a riot of colour and magic, they overwhelm their foes with sizzling spell-bolts, lashing talons and gouts of blistering wyrdflame, exulting as their hapless victims are consumed by the fires of change.

The Disciples of Tzeentch: Daemons are as bizarre and unpredictable as the god from whom they were spawned. Capering across the battlefield in a riot of colour and magic, they overwhelm their foes with sizzling spell-bolts, lashing talons and gouts of blistering wyrdflame, exulting as their hapless victims are consumed by the fires of change.
content: RunemarkAbilityTzeentch Daemons[Double] Locus of Sorcery: Add half the value of this ability (rounding up) to the Strength characteristic of the next missile attack action made by this fighter in this activation.Tzeentch DaemonsAgile[Triple] Latching Bite: Add half the value of this ability (rounding up) to the Strength characteristic of the next attack action made by this fighter in this activation. In addition, until the end of the battle round, the target fighter cannot make move or disengage actions.Tzeentch DaemonsBerserker[Quad] Capricious Warpflame: Allocate a number of damage points equal to the value of this ability to all visible enemy fighters within 3" of this fighter.

meta_description: The Disciples of Tzeentch: Daemons are as bizarre and unpredictable as the god from whom they were spawned. Capering across the battlefield in a riot of colour and magic, they overwhelm their foes with sizzling spell-bolts, lashing talons and gouts of blistering wyrdflame, exulting as their hapless victims are consumed by the fires of change.
content: RunemarkAbilityTzeentch DaemonsHero[Double] Blue Fire: Pick a visible enemy fighter within 9" of this fighter and roll 2 dice. For each roll of 4-5, allocate 1 damage point to that fighter. For each roll of 6, allocate a number of damage points to that fighter equal to the value of this ability.Tzeentch DaemonsHero[Triple] Master of Destiny: Until the end of the battle round, add the value of this ability to the value of other abilities (to a maximum of 6) used by friendly fighters within 12" of this fighter.Tzeentch DaemonsHeroDestroyer[Triple] Pink Fire: Pick a visible enemy fighter within 9" of this fighter and roll 9 dice. For each roll of 4+, allocate 1 damage point to that fighter.

meta_description: The Disciples of Tzeentch: Daemons are as bizarre and unpredictable as the god from whom they were spawned. Capering across the battlefield in a riot of colour and magic, they overwhelm their foes with sizzling spell-bolts, lashing talons and gouts of blistering wyrdflame, exulting as their hapless victims are consumed by the fires of change.
content: RunemarkAbilityEphilim's PandaemoniumFly[Double] Hypnotise: Pick an enemy fighter within 6" of this fighter. That enemy fighter makes a move action directly towards this fighter, as if they were jumping, up to a number of inches equal to their Move characteristic.Ephilim's Pandaemonium[Double] Sudden Warp-portal: Pick another friendly fighter with the Ephilim's Pandaemonium (Ephilim's Pandaemonium) runemark within 12" of this fighter. Make a note of that fighter's location, then remove that fighter from the battlefield, and immediately set them up within 3" of this fighter. Then remove this fighter from the battlefield, and set them up within 3" of the other friendly fighter's original location.Ephilim's PandaemoniumBerserker[Triple] Engulfing Bite: Add 2 to the Attacks and Strength characteristics of the next melee attack action made by this fighter in this activation. In addition, add 2 to the damage points allocated to enemy fighters by each critical hit from that attack action.Ephilim's PandaemoniumHero[Quad] Summoned Abomination: Pick a friendly fighter with the Ephilim's Pandaemonium (Ephilim's Pandaemonium) runemark that has been taken down. Set up that fighter on a platform or the battlefield floor, wholly within 3" of this fighter. That fighter no longer counts as being taken down and has no damage points allocated to it.

content: A congregation of Tzeentchian daemons led by Ephilim, the erstwhile apprentice to a powerful Gaunt Summoner. A keen daemonologist, Ephilim has plucked many rare creatures out of the aether to do their bidding – some with far too many mouths, some with far too many legs, and, disturbingly, some with no legs at all. Bladeborn fightersEphilim's Pandaemonium are from Warhammer Underworlds, so adhere to Bladeborn rules.

content: Q: The abilities ‘Split', and ‘Split Again' add new fighters to your warband. How does this interact with victory cards that relate to the number of fighters in a battle group and/ or warband? A: The new fighters are added to your warband but are not part of any battle group. Q: If a Horror uses either the ‘Split' or the ‘Split Again' ability, can the new Horrors activate in that battle round? A: Yes. Q: The Screamer of Tzeentch's ‘Latching Bite' ability refers to a ‘target fighter'. Who is the target fighter? A: The ‘target fighter' is the target of the melee attack action made by the Screamer of Tzeentch that used the ability. Q: A Screamer of Tzeentch uses the ‘Latching Bite' ability. Does that Screamer have to make a melee attack action in order to prevent the target of that attack action from making move or disengage actions? A: Yes.

content: White Dwarf #483Warcry Core Book 2019D6Origin1Vow of Silence: Your warband has taken their ritualistic maiming one step further in devotion to the Great Gatherer. Their severed vocal chords no longer produce any noise at all.2Tree-runners: Your warband prefers to stalk the highest trees within the Gnarlwood's canopy.3Gargoyles: Your warband is used to spending hours, even days, in the same vantage spot.4Taint of the Eightpoints: Your warband once fought for Archaon's favour within the Bloodwind Spoil.5Duplicitous: Your warband prefers to lead enemies into traps before finishing them off.6Zealous Killers: Your warriors seek out and kill those marked for death by the Great Gatherer.D6Origin1Trophy-takers: Your warband carves trophies from the flesh of those they have slain.2Roof-runners: Your warband stalks the roofs and spires of ruined cities.3Gargoyles: You and your warriors are used to spending hours, even days, in the same vantage spot.4Shades of the Eyrie: Your warband was once tasked with defending the Cabal's mountaintop stronghold.5Infiltrators: There is no locked door or secured stronghold that your warband cannot breach.6Zealous Killers: Your warriors seek out and kill those marked for death by the Great Gatherer.

content: D6Background1Eyetaker: This warrior plucks out the eyes of their victims as keepsakes.2Patient Cunning: This warrior always waits for the perfect moment to strike.3Death From Above: This warrior prefers to end their foe's life with a single, brutal strike from on high.4Realistic: This warrior is perfectly willing to retreat in order to win another day.5Avaricious: This warrior will risk everything for a glittering prize.6Ear-splitting Shriek: This warrior unleashes a piercing cry as they leap in to attack.

meta_description: Source: Warcry Tome of Champions 2019 (out of print)
content: D6Origin1Devoted: These warriors wholeheartedly believe their leader to be Slaanesh's rightful heir.2Egomaniacs: Each member of this warband believes themselves to be the finest of all warriors.3On the Hunt: Your warriors tirelessly hunt for their lost god, never halting in the chase.4Soulseekers: Your warriors are forever seeking new unfortunate mortals to torment.5Frenzied Despoilers: Your warriors desire nothing more than to spread vice and corruption.6Addicted to Slaughter: The rush of killing has become an addiction for these Hedonites.

meta_description: Source: Warcry Tome of Champions 2019 (out of print)
content: D6Background1Heir to the Sinful Throne: This warrior believes themselves destined to take the place of absent Slaanesh.2Out of Favour: This warrior was once a Herald or warleader amongst their Host, and they wish to ascend to glory once more.3Dark Muse: This warrior has led many artists and philosophers to a dark, obsession-fuelled end.4Alluring: Those who face this warrior are struck dumb by their loathsome and strange beauty.5Perfectionist: This warrior is obsessed with delivering perfect killing strikes.6Hedonistic Exemplar: This warrior is fanatically devoted to a particular form of vice.

meta_description: Source: Warcry Tome of Champions 2019 (out of print)
content: D6Origin1Suicide Mission: Your clan's ruler has sent you on this mission thoroughly expecting you to fail.2Realmstone Hunters: Your warband is searching for realmstone deposits to power the death-dealing contraptions of the Skryre clans.3Plague Seekers: Your warband zealously hunts for ingredients to brew one of the Great Plagues.4Killing Shadows: Your warriors are talented assassins, hunting down those marked for death.5To Mould Monsters: At the command of your masters, your warband is looking for prime specimens to create new and terrible monsters.6Natural-born Schemers: Your warriors are constantly looking for ways to undermine one another.

meta_description: Source: Warcry Tome of Champions 2019 (out of print)
content: D6Background1Black Hunger: This warrior's furious metabolism demands he constantly feed or otherwise perish.2Warlock's Favourite: This warrior has been ‘rewarded' by the Skryre warlocks with new – and untested – inventions and weapons.3The Withered Word: This warrior's squeaking oratory inspires a form of courage in their minions.4Shade of Murder: No wall or gatehouse can stop this warrior from reaching their prey.5It's Alive!: The mutated rat-beasts raised by this warrior are truly horrific examples of their kind.6Vicious Fighter: This warrior is a surprisingly courageous fighter – for a skaven, at least.

meta_description: Source: Predators and Prey (out of print)
content: D6Origin1Crimson Survivalists: These warriors are used to hunting through even the deadliest environments; their raw rage keeps them warm on the harshest nights.2Marked Territory: Within a land claimed by Khorne's warhordes, this pack has carved out their own stalking ground. Woe betide any who intrude upon their domain.3The Master's Hounds: This warband are the personal huntsmen of a powerful Khornate lord, and eagerly collect the remains of their enemies to pile at his feet.4Punishers of the Pack: Other followers of Khorne who have earned their god's displeasure are this warband's favoured prey.5The Beast Beneath: Though perhaps even they do not remember it, these warriors were once worshippers of the God-King, their souls set aflame by a Flesh Hound's roar.6A Hunt beyond Sanity: It is said that these ferocious warriors have even prowled the blasted lands of Khorne's domain. They are far too deranged to confirm or deny it.

meta_description: Source: Predators and Prey (out of print)
content: D6Background1One with the Monster: This warrior is entirely at home in the Chaos-ravaged wilds; even the merest hint of civilisation drives them into a furious rage.2Cacophony of Slaughter: When this warrior throws back their head and roars, nearby canines:** especially those of daemonic provenance:** cannot help but add to the din.3Cursed Appetite -This warrior refuses to consume anything except the raw flesh of enemies they have personally slaughtered.4Shadow of the Hound: This warrior was 'awakened' by a howl loosed from one of Karanak's three throats:** a sure mark of greatness.5Hunter of the Hated Arcane: This champion armour is hung with bloody skins and pelts ripped from sorcerers of many different species.6Inevitable Vengeance: So potent is this warrior's sense for any who defy Khorne that they can track them even through the realmgate portals.

meta_description: Source: Warcry Tome of Champions 2019 (out of print)
content: D6Origin1To the Master's Tune: Your warband is entangled in a complex web of plots and schemes.2The Hidden Threat: Your warriors lurk amidst enemy territory, waiting for the perfect moment to strike.3Seeking Ascension: Your warriors wish to mutate into new and wondrous forms – or have done so already.4A Foolish Pact: Your warband once made an alliance with a local warlord, but you have long since betrayed them for personal gain.5Duplicitous: Your warriors delight in spreading confusion and misrule wherever they go.6Pyromaniacal: These warriors seek to bring Tzeentch's flickering fires to all they encounter.

meta_description: Source: Warcry Tome of Champions 2019 (out of print)
content: D6Background1Cackling Maniac: Even by the standards of Tzeentch's servants, this warrior is truly mad.2Wheels within Wheels: This warrior is particularly adept at manipulating others to serve their will.3Child of Flux: This warrior seeks new mutations, no matter how impractical, with a rabid devotion.4Aethereater: This daemon seeks to find:** and devour:** powerful manifestations of magic.5A Wicked Jest: This daemon has a cruel sense of humour and delights in tormenting mortals.6Wreathed in Warpflame: Bright changefire constantly surrounds this cackling daemon.

content: D6Origin1Alchemists: Your warriors specialise in the brewing of mind-altering potions and hallucinogenic bombs.2Agents of Nochseed: Your warriors take the guise of diplomats and traders, infiltrating rival cities.3Deceptive Duellists: You are students of the College of Mirrors, Nochseed's premier martial academy.4Thrallmasters: Your warband was charged with recruiting worthy battle-thralls for the Cypher Lords.5Secret Keepers: Your warband has stared into the depths of madness, and gleaned prophetic secrets.6King-killers: You and your warriors are experts in the art of instigating coups and violent revolts

meta_description: Source: Warcry Core Book 2019 (out of print)
content: D6Background1Keen Wit: This warrior is an urbane and charismatic presence, always ready with a cutting quip.2Dark Obsession: This warrior offers a facade of civility, but indulges in an appalling secret vice.3Master of the Eight Forms: This warrior is a peerless swordfighter, and takes every chance to prove it.4Unsettling Gaze: One gaze into this warrior's eyes reveals an inner well of madness.5Connoisseur: This warrior seeks only the finest things:** whether thralls, food or opponents.6Voice-thrower: This warrior can project their voice, frightening and unsettling their foes.

meta_description: Source: Warcry Red Harvest (out of print)
content: D6Origin1Divine Visitations: This warband has been guided on its latest quest by visions that they believe stem from the gods themselves.2Vow of the Soulfeast: The warriors of this warband have sworn to feed the gods' soul-hunger by slaughtering enemies of as many species and creeds as they can.3Live Off the Land: Exiles from their former tribe, this warband is well used to subsistence living and taking what they need through brutal raids.4Vow of Dark Tribute: Having sworn an oath before one of the mighty Varanguard, this warband proudly serves dread Archaon in acquiring varanite.5Truly Fearless: This warband deliberately seeks out the most harrowing crucibles of battle in which to prove their valour.6Purest Savagery: These warriors move unseen and unheard, silently tracking their prey.

meta_description: Source: Warcry Red Harvest (out of print)
content: D6Background1Strong Right Hand: This warrior serves directly beneath a powerful Chieftain or Warqueen, enforcing their will amongst the ruinous tribes.2Grisly Excesses: Such is this warrior's savage lust for violence that they will continue to hack apart and mutilate their foes even after they are dead.3Glory at Any Cost: Even other Darkoath stand in awe of the lengths that this warrior will go to in order to fulfil their vows.4Marked for Greatness: The tribal shamans watch this warrior closely, muttering that the vows they have fulfilled have already drawn the gaze of the gods.5Ancestral Shame: One of this warrior's kin failed to fulfil an oath to the gods, a shame the leader now fights to rise above.6The Craven Arcane: This warrior does not trust any who wield the power of sorcery, not even those sworn to the Dark Gods.

content: D6Origin1Overseers: Your warband oversaw the labouring of the Iron Golems' thralls with ruthless cruelty.2Weaponsmiths: The blades and hammers crafted by your warband are of masterful quality.3Onyx Guard: Your warband were elite guardians of the Onyx Fist, the Golems' Dreadhold.4Breaching Party: Your warriors are experts at smashing a path through enemy fortifications.5Voice of the Golem: It was your duty to enforce the collection of tribute from the Golems' vassal states.6Veterans of the Ash War: Your warband took part in the battle to recapture the dragon Axranathos, when the creature broke free.

meta_description: Source: Warcry Core Book 2019 (out of print)
content: D6Background1Blood of Mithraxes: This warrior is a proud relation of High Overlord Mithraxes, ruler of the Iron Golems.2Drillmaster:: This warrior is responsible for maintaining cohesion and discipline in the ranks of the Golems.3Dragon-marked: This warrior was burned by the fires of Axranathos, and wears these marks proudly.4Cruel Tyrant: This warrior commands through sheer brutality.5Vengeful: This warrior never forgives a slight, and seeks vengeance with single-minded ferocity.6Unstoppable Force: This warrior eschews tactical cunning, preferring to face every threat head-on.

meta_description: Source: Rot and Ruin (out of print)
content: D6Origin1Bringers of Fire: It is the task of these warriors to scorch the land, incinerating any hint of life to pave the way for Hashut's legions.2Shrine-breakers: These warriors strike at the heart of their foes by targeting shrines, temples and symbols of non-Hashutite faiths.3Taskmasters: It is the task of these warriors to ensure that Hashut's will is carried out, and punish those who fail him.4Infernal Brand: These warriors have been branded by one of Hashut's high-ranking priests, and serve their merciless master without question.5Heralds of Desolation: These warriors advance ahead of the Horns' main force, eliminating scouts, watchtowers and other advanced positions.6Siegebreakers: These Horns are experts atshattering the walls of great fortresses, opening breaches to let their comrades through

meta_description: Source: Rot and Ruin (out of print)
content: D6Background1Fist of the Prophet: This warrior serves one of Hashut's merciless sorcerer-prophets, killing and destroying in their name.2Pitiless Tyrant: This warrior delights in crushing those who cannot defend themselves, for the weak do not deserve to exist.3Enemy of Nature: This warrior despises the teeming wildness of nature, and delights in setting it aflame.4Breath of Cinders: Infernal cinders constantly spill from the nostrils of this warrior's hashutaar war-mask.5Unstoppable Charge: When this warrior thunders forward, there is no force in the realms that can hold them back.6Ruinous Exemplar: This warrior is marked with runic brands that tell of a long history of destruction and slaughter.

meta_description: Source: Warcry Tome of Champions 2019 (out of print)
content: D6Origin1The Path to Ruin: Through acts of carnage, your warband has begun to attract the gaze of the gods.2Dark Champions: These warriors do not miss an opportunity to prove their prowess in battle.3The Dark Arts: Your warband serves the tribal shamans by seeking out arcane treasures.4Spreaders of Corruption: You and your warriors seek to spread the taint of Chaos far and wide.5Host of Conquerors: Your warband has fought among the conquering armies of the Everchosen himself.6Godtouched: Your warriors are deeply engaged in the worship of one particular aspect of Chaos.

meta_description: Source: Warcry Tome of Champions 2019 (out of print)
content: D6Background1The Mark of Chaos: This warrior has earned the mark of one of the Ruinous Powers.2Infamous Warleader: This warrior's dark charisma sees many would-be champions flock to their banner.3Infernal Knowledge: This warrior has fought for many years and has seen much of the dark power of Chaos.4Touched by the Divine: It is said that daemonic power courses through this warrior's veins.5Brand of the Everchosen: This warrior bears Archaon's own eight-pointed mark on their flesh or armour.6Exalted: Bloody omens suggest that this warrior is destined for greatness in the eyes of the gods.

page_title: Scions of the Flame Background Tables
meta_description: Source: Warcry Core Book 2019 (out of print)
content: D6Origin1Proselytisers: Your warband has travelled far across Aqshy, preaching its brutal creed.2Ragerock Hunters: Your warband was responsible for seeking out deposits of precious ragerock.3Ashen Ones: Your warband reveres the Ashen King, a legendary servant of the Ever-Raging Flame.4Inquisitors: Your warband roots out any souls who doubt the primacy of the Flame.5Tunnel Fighters: Veterans of many underground battles, your warriors are experts in closequarters combat.6True Fanatics: Utterly devoted to the worship of the Flame, death means nothing to your warriors.

page_title: Scions of the Flame Background Tables
meta_description: Source: Warcry Core Book 2019 (out of print)
section_title: Scions of the Flame Leader Background
content: D6Background1Booming Voice: This warrior's voice rings out thunderously across the field of battle.2Reborn in the Flames: This warrior has survived immolation, and emerged all the stronger.3Hatred of Duardin: This warrior despises the covetous duardin, who lay claim to vast stocks of ragerock.4Quick to Anger: This warrior is hot-blooded and aggressive, a living embodiment of Aqshy.5Holy Warrior: This warrior seeks out rival preachers to purge with cleansing flame.6Orator: In battle, this warrior recites the creed of the Ever-Raging Flame.

meta_description: Source: Warcry Core Book 2019 (out of print)
content: D10First NameD10Last Name1Norsc1of the Bloody Shield2Ghraddus2Skullsplitter3Kalenya3the Black4Banusk4Bane of Cowards5Traskor5the Titan6Kroll6Heart-eater7Sharrak7the Spider8Talya8the Undefeated9Orresk9Spinebreaker10Nemetta10Splithelm

meta_description: Source: Warcry Core Book 2019 (out of print)
content: D6Origin1Errant Gladiators: Your warriors butchered their guards and broke free of the arena.2Bodyguards: Your warband has earned coin and prestige as hired spears for several warlords.3Crowd Favourites: Your warband's reputation for savagery and showmanship precedes them.4Hunted: Your warband has earned the ire of powerful foes who seek their destruction.5Monster Hunters: Mere mortals no longer offer a challenge. Your warriors seek greater prey.6Freed Ones: You put on such a spectacle in the arena that an influential Chaos Lord secured your release.

meta_description: Source: Warcry Core Book 2019 (out of print)
content: D6Background1Breathtaking Arrogance: As far as this warrior is concerned, no enemy is their equal in battle.2Death Tally: This warrior marks every kill they have ever made upon their flesh or armour.3Disfigured: This warrior has suffered a horrendous wound that renders them a most fearful sight.4Vaunting Ambition: This warrior seeks power with a furious intensity.5Signature Kill: This warrior has a favoured method of despatching their foes.6Oppressor: This warrior likes nothing more than brutalising those weaker than they.

content: Change to the Pack Tactics ability.

meta_description: Released: May 2023, Nightmare Quest boxed set.
content: Optional RuleBattle Traits are optional rules that add additional flavour to a warband. All fighters must share this runemark to be eligible.There are also Battle Traits for each Grand Alliance. Trait RunemarkTraitRoyal BeastflayersRoyal BeastflayersThe Hounds Bay: After an enemy fighter has been taken down by a melee attack action made by a friendly fighter with this battle trait that has the Beast (Beast) runemark, pick another fighter in your warband. That fighter can make a bonus move action of up to 3”.

meta_description: Released: May 2023, Nightmare Quest boxed set.
content: RunemarkReactionRoyal BeastflayersTenacious Hunters: After a visible enemy fighter within 1" of this fighter makes a disengage action, allocate D3 damage points to that fighter.

meta_description: Released: May 2023, Nightmare Quest boxed set.
content: RunemarkAbilityRoyal Beastflayers[Double] Feed the Hounds: A fighter can only use this ability if an enemy fighter was taken down by an attack action made by them this activation. Remove a number of damage points from each other friendly fighter within 3" of this fighter, up to the value of this ability.Royal BeastflayersBrute[Double] Sound the Hunt: Pick a number of visible friendly fighters equal to half the value of this ability (rounding up) that are within 3" of this fighter. Those fighters can each make a bonus move action of a number of inches equal to the value of this ability.Royal BeastflayersBeast[Double] Clambering Horrors: Until the end of this fighter's activation, do not count the vertical distance moved when this fighter is climbing.Royal BeastflayersChampion[Triple] Unleash the Hounds: Until the end of the battle round, add half the value of this ability (rounding up) to the Move characteristic of friendly fighters with the Royal Beastflayers runemark (Royal Beastflayers) while they make a move action that starts within 3" of this fighter.Royal BeastflayersHero[Triple] Pack Tactics: Choose an enemy fighter that is visible to this fighter. Allocate a number of damage points to that enemy fighter equal to twice the number of friendly fighters with the Royal Beastflayers runemark (Royal Beastflayers) within 3" of them.'Royal BeastflayersHero[Quad] No Rest till our Quest is Done: Until the end of the battle round, add 2 to the Strength characteristic of melee attack actions made by visible friendly fighters while they are within 6" of this fighter.

meta_description: Source: Stealth and Stone (out of print)
content: D6Origin1Stonecursed Ones: These warriors are almost entirely consumed by the petrifying curse that plagues the Jade Obelisk, and their every motion is a creaking agony.2Descendants of Mephrobaal: These warriors trace their lineage to the great Mason-King that led the Jade Obelisk into damnation.3Idolarc Sculptors: It is the duty of these warriors to erect monuments fashioned in the image of an ldolarc, in the hope that these statues might come to life.4Stone-shapers: These warriors are experts in sculpting weapons and armour from the petrified flesh of their slain foes.5Jade Exiles: These warriors have been cast out from the Jade Obelisk and must earn their place again with a worthy offering to the Speaker in the Stone.6Embittered: These warriors despise the Speaker in the Stone and their current predicament, but their petrifying curse robs them of the chance to rebel.

meta_description: Source: Stealth and Stone (out of print)
content: D6Background1Grand Nephrite: The leader has consumed powdered jade with every meal for years, and their skin has taken something of its hue.2Sculptor of Foes: Toe leader relishes carving the flesh of the enemy into more pleasing, linear shapes, the better to honour Mephrobaal.3Cadence of the Chisel: The ritual dagger of this leader strikes with the methodical rhythm of the sculptor's finest tools, over and again.4Maskless: This leader forsakes the mask of jade. Her expression fixed, she has become one with the stone, just as the stone is one with her.5Daughter of Jade: This leader claims to have hatched from a giant egg of pure nephrite, and given the gleam in her eye, few care to dispute it.6Iconoclairvoyant: The leader can unnerringly find her way to the statues of gods, the better to destroy them.

meta_description: Released: August 2024, Briar and Bone boxed set.
content: Optional RuleBattle Traits are optional rules that add additional flavour to a warband. All fighters must share this runemark to be eligible.There are also Battle Traits for each Grand Alliance. Trait RunemarkTraitTeratic CohortTeratic CohortPredatory Outriders: Before deployment, you can pick a friendly battle group that is not In reserve. Fighters from that battle group can deploy within 6” of their deployment point, but if they do so, must be deployed more than 6” from each enemy fighter.

meta_description: Released: August 2024, Briar and Bone boxed set.
content: RunemarkReactionTeratic CohortPredatory Ravage: A fighter with the Teratic Cohort runemark can make this reaction after all the damage points from an enemy fighter's melee attack action have been allocated to them. The reacting fighter can make a melee attack action that targets the attacker. When a fighter uses this reaction, you can discard one of your saved wild dice. If you do not, subtract 1 from the attacks characteristic of that attack action, and subtract 1 from the damage points allocated by each hit and critical hit from that attack action (to a minimum of 1).

meta_description: Released: August 2024, Briar and Bone boxed set.
content: RunemarkAbilityTeratic CohortElite[Double] Bestial Leaps: This fighter can make a bonus move action of up to 3", and can fly when they do so. When a fighter uses this ability, you can discard one of your saved wild dice. If you do so, this fighter can make a bonus move action of up to 4" instead, and can fly when they do so.Teratic Cohort[Double] Eyes of Katakross: Roll a number of dice equal to the value of this ability. For each roll of 5+, add one wild dice to your saved wild dice.Teratic CohortElite[Double] Nadirite Strike: Add 2 to the Attacks characteristic of the next melee attack action made by this fighter. When a fighter uses this ability, you can discard two of your saved wild dice. If you do so, critical hits are scored on a 5+ for that attack action.Teratic CohortBeast[Triple] Artery-severing Strike: A fighter can use this ability only if an enemy fighter has been allocated 1 or more damage points by a melee attack action made by them this activation. Until the end of the battle round, each time that enemy fighter makes a reaction, or an action other than wait, roll a number of dice equal to the value of this ability. For each roll of 2+, allocate 1 damage point to that fighter.Teratic Cohort[Triple] Unleashed Rage: One critical hit from the next melee attack action made by this fighter in this activation scores 1 additional critical hit. When a fighter uses this ability, you can discard two of your saved wild dice. If you do so, one critical hit from the next melee attack action made by this fighter in this activation scores 2 additional critical hits instead.Teratic CohortElite[Quad] Savagery Unbound: Each friendly fighter with the Teratic Cohort runemark in this fighter's battle group can make a bonus move action. When a fighter uses this ability, you can discard two of your saved wild dice. If you do, each friendly fighter with the Teratic Cohort runemark can make a bonus move action instead.

meta_description: Released: August 2022.
content: Optional RuleBattle Traits are optional rules that add additional flavour to a warband. All fighters must share this runemark to be eligible.There are also Battle Traits for each Grand Alliance. Trait RunemarkTraitChaos LegionnairesChaos LegionnairesMaster Manipulators: When determining who has the initiative, but not when seizing the initiative, if there is a tie, do not roll off. Instead the player of this warband chooses which tied player has the initiative. If multiple warbands have this battle trait, the players of those warbands roll off, and the winner chooses which tied player has the initiative.

meta_description: Released: August 2022.
content: RunemarkReactionChaos LegionnairesCall to Arms: A fighter can make this reaction when an enemy fighter ends their move action visible to this fighter and within 3" of this fighter. Pick another friendly fighter within 3" of this fighter. Tht fighter makes a bonus move action. After that move action that fighter must be within 1" of the enemy fighter that made the move action.

meta_description: Released: August 2022.
content: RunemarkAbilityChaos LegionnairesBulwark[Double] Shield Ram: Until the end of this fighter's activation, the next time this fighter finishes a move action within 1" of an enemy fighter, pick a visible enemy fighter within 1" of this fighter and roll a dice. On a 4-5, allocate 1 damage point to that fighter. On a 6, allocate a number of damage points to that fighter equal to the value of this ability.Chaos LegionnairesHero[Double] Dark Pact: Pick a visible fighter within 12" of this fighter. Roll a number of dice equal to the value of this ability. For each 3+, allocate 1 damage point to that fighter. After all of the dice have been rolled, allocate D3 damage points to this fighter.Chaos LegionnairesBrute[Double] Brutal Swing: Add half the value of this ability (rounding up) to the damage points allocated by each critical hit from the next attack action made by this fighter this activation.Chaos LegionnairesBerserker[Triple] Headlong Charge: A fighter can use this ability only if they are within 2" of an enemy fighter and have made 2 move actions this activation. This fighter makes a bonus attack action with its spear.Chaos LegionnairesHero[Triple] The True Master's Favour: A fighter can use this ability only if an enemy fighter has been taken down by an attack action made by them this activation. Until the end of the battle round, add 1 to the Attacks characteristic of melee attack action made by friendly fighters while they are within 9" of this fighter.Chaos Legionnaires[Quad] Wrath of the First Prince: This fighter makes a bonus attack action. In addition, a number of hits scored by that attack action equal to half the value of this ability (rounding up) become critical hits instead. For example, if the value of the ability was 6, 3 hits score by that attack action would become critical hits instead.
